topic: Packet 015 save-flow planner-first trim + cross-packet residual sweep

answeredQuestions.verdict: atomic-index-memory.ts, create-record.ts, and thin-continuity-record.ts are unchanged, but content-router.ts gained new Tier 3 gating and manual-review control flow, so the load-bearing set is not bit-level clean.

Full-auto still reaches the atomic writer, Tier 3 routing, quality-loop auto-fix, reconsolidation, enrichment, chunking, graph refresh, and reindex, but it no longer enforces POST_SAVE_FINGERPRINT inside the canonical validation path.

answeredQuestions.text: Flag wire completeness (4 new env flags)
answeredQuestions.verdict: All four new save-path flags are live: each one is read and drives a real branch in routing, quality-loop, reconsolidation, or post-insert enrichment.
All three follow-up APIs are implemented, route into real runtime logic, and are re-exported from api/index.ts.
Spec-doc legality checks still block and score-heavy checks remain advisories, but template-contract violations are misclassified as planner advisories instead of blockers.
All eight route categories and Tier 1/Tier 2 router logic stayed intact; only the default Tier 3 invocation path changed.

The refactor leaves one real success-shaped no-op in post-insert enrichment status reporting and one documented future stub for `hybrid` planner mode, but reconsolidation and quality-loop gating are still live.

The required focused suites all pass and core planner/full-auto branches are covered, but deferred follow-up execution for enrichment backfill and reindex remains largely shape-tested instead of runtime-tested.
Command docs match runtime, but packet 015 spec/plan still cite old follow-up tool names and ENV_REFERENCE overstates `hybrid` as a real planner mode.

Residual deprecated-surface checks, lockfile verification, required builds, and the standing regression script all pass; the remaining cross-packet issue is changelog honesty around `hybrid` support and load-bearing router preservation.

findings.title: Load-bearing router file carries new control-flow, not just trim plumbing
findings.summary: Packet 015 introduces env-driven Tier 3 gating and new manual-review control flow inside content-router.ts while the other three load-bearing files stayed unchanged.
findings.remediation: Document content-router.ts as an explicit preservation exception or move the Tier 3 default-disable behavior outside the load-bearing preservation set.

findings.title: Full-auto fallback drops the pre-015 POST_SAVE_FINGERPRINT safety rule
findings.summary: The canonical full-auto path still builds a postSavePlan, but its validator call no longer includes the POST_SAVE_FINGERPRINT rule that pre-015 enforced by default.
findings.remediation: Restore the full-auto validator call so POST_SAVE_FINGERPRINT remains active on canonical fallback writes.

findings.title: Planner-ready responses downgrade template-contract failures into advisories
findings.summary: The planner response path emits template-contract violations as advisories even though the save pipeline still rejects those violations outside warn-only mode.
findings.remediation: Promote invalid template-contract results to planner blockers, except where the existing templateContractBypassed exception explicitly applies.

findings.title: Planner-default enrichment skip returns success-shaped status
findings.summary: When save-time enrichment is deferred in planner-default mode, the wrapper returns all-true enrichment flags so the response surface cannot tell that enrichment never ran.
findings.remediation: Return an explicit deferred/skipped enrichment status or warning when planner-default mode bypasses the enrichment pipeline.

findings.title: `hybrid` planner mode is exposed but currently behaves exactly like `plan-only`
findings.summary: The runtime accepts `hybrid` as a planner mode, but the active branches only distinguish `full-auto` from every other value, so `hybrid` is a documented future stub rather than a real mode.
findings.remediation: Remove `hybrid` from the active contract until implemented, or add a dedicated mixed-flow branch so the mode is behaviorally distinct.

findings.title: Deferred follow-up helpers lack execution-level coverage
findings.summary: Planner tests assert `reindex` and `enrich` follow-up actions are advertised, but the underlying `reindexSpecDocs()`, `runEnrichmentBackfill()`, and planner-gated enrichment helper are not exercised end to end across planner/apply mode boundaries.
findings.remediation: Add runtime tests that execute deferred reindex/enrichment follow-ups and assert the planner-default skipped branch differs from explicit full-auto execution.

findings.title: Packet docs still reference pre-015 follow-up tool names
findings.summary: The packet spec and plan still say planner responses surface `memory_index_scan` and `refreshGraphMetadataForSpecFolder`, but runtime now emits `reindexSpecDocs` and `refreshGraphMetadata`.
findings.remediation: Update packet 015 docs to use the shipped follow-up API names: refreshGraphMetadata, reindexSpecDocs, and runEnrichmentBackfill.

findings.title: ENV reference presents `hybrid` as a supported planner mode even though it is still a future stub
findings.summary: The env table lists `hybrid` alongside `plan-only` and `full-auto`, but runtime only distinguishes `full-auto` from non-`full-auto` and the source comment explicitly labels hybrid as future mixed flows.
findings.remediation: Mark hybrid as reserved/future in ENV_REFERENCE.md or remove it from the documented public mode set until implemented.

findings.title: Phase 015 release notes overstate hybrid support and unchanged load-bearing preservation
findings.summary: The v3.4.1.0 changelog describes `hybrid` as a real planner mode and marks the router/load-bearing verification as unchanged, but runtime still treats hybrid as a future stub and the router diff is behavioral.
findings.remediation: Amend the changelog to describe hybrid as reserved/future and to state that router contract parity held while default Tier 3/manual-review control flow changed.
